raw_material_chokepoints.alternatives: Asahi Kasei/Celgard (Japan/US), Toray, SK IE Tech

china_export_weaponization.action: China restricted LFP and LMFP cathode technology exports
DRC imposed cobalt export quotas (96,600 MT annually, less than half of 2024 output)

key_finding: No 'NDAA-compliant' cell is truly China-free at the raw material level — the question is degree of exposure and whether chokepoints can be rapidly diversified

regulatory_timeline.scope: Prohibits Chinese-sourced critical drone components, but batteries NOT explicitly listed
Bans DoD procurement from 6 named Chinese companies: CATL, BYD, EVE Energy, Envision, Gotion, Hithium
Extends ban to all Foreign Entities of Concern

emerging_technologies.name: Silicon-anode lithium-ion
emerging_technologies.timeline: Available now for defense; broad commercial 2027-2028
emerging_technologies.ndaa_path: Strongest near-term — US companies building domestic manufacturing with defense funding

emerging_technologies.name: Lithium-sulfur
emerging_technologies.supply_chain_independence: Complete — no nickel, cobalt, manganese, or graphite

emerging_technologies.name: Solid-state batteries
leaders: Solid Power (electrolyte licensing to BMW/SK On)
Samsung SDI (500 Wh/kg mass production planned 2027)
emerging_technologies.drone_exception: Factorial Energy pivoted to drones/robotics; ESOX Group claims production-ready cell at CES 2026

emerging_technologies.name: Hydrogen fuel cells

providers.name: Intelligent Energy (UK)
providers.milestone: UK's first hydrogen BVLOS flight Nov 2025

emerging_technologies.limitation: Requires hydrogen infrastructure; cost-effective only for drones >15 kg MTOW

emerging_technologies.name: Sodium-ion
emerging_technologies.note: 60-70% of lithium-ion energy density. Natron Energy (US) ceased operations Sep 2025.
emerging_technologies.timeline: Not viable for most drone applications

pouch_cell_gap.problem: Most multirotor drones use LiPo pouch cells. Zero commercial-scale US production exists.
pouch_cell_gap.cannot_solve: Molicel and Samsung make cylindrical cells, not pouch
pouch_cell_gap.best_hope: Badland Batteries $50M DoD APFIT factory in Fargo, targeting 2026 production
pouch_cell_gap.investment_gap: Current DoD programs total tens of millions vs CHIPS Act-scale $50-100B needed
pouch_cell_gap.realistic_timeline: US/allied production to match Chinese capacity: 2029-2030 minimum

strategic_conclusions: Molicel is a single point of failure for allied high-drain cylindrical supply, worsened by July 2025 fire
No non-Chinese pouch cell supply exists at scale — most drone designs lack NDAA-compliant power source
China controls 70-98% of every upstream material, so even allied cells carry residual Chinese exposure
2027-2028 NDAA compliance deadlines will arrive before domestic supply catches up
Most practical 2026-2028 strategy: Molicel/Samsung cylindrical + accelerated Amprius + design adaptation away from Chinese pouch formats
